key_insight: A unified multi-scale ML framework can jointly model chemical, spin, and structural disorder in alloys and accurately predict key phase transitions.
engineering_relevance: The approach enables more reliable design and screening of complex magnetic alloys where multiple disorder types control thermodynamic and structural behavior.
method: Use graph neural networks and machine-learning interatomic potentials to represent alloy energetics.
Couple Monte Carlo and molecular dynamics to sample chemical, spin, and lattice fluctuations across scales.
Apply the framework to Fe-Co-C as a prototypical multi-disorder system.
evidence_signal: The model predicts Fe-Co order-disorder transition and melting temperatures near experimental values (1,000 K vs 1,006 K; 1,690 K vs ~1,700 K) and captures tetragonal-to-nearly-cubic transitions in Fe-Co-C.
watch_item: Performance may depend on high-quality training data and careful coupling of magnetic, chemical, and structural degrees of freedom for new alloy systems.
paper_title: A Multi-Scale Machine Learning Framework for Coupled Chemical, Spin, and Structural Disorder in Alloys
